docs.title: Datatrail CLI
docs.text:
Installation

The Datatrail CLI can be installed from PYPI using pip:

pip install datatrail-cli

It can also be installed from source using git and poetry:

git clone ssh+git://git@github.com/chimefrb/datatrail-cli
cd datatrail-cli

# If you don't have Poetry:
pip install poetry

poetry install --without docs

docs.title: A CLI to interact with files registered in Datatrail
docs.text: In order to fully utilise this CLI, you must have an account with CANFAR and access to the either of the following groups: chime-frb-ro or chime-frb-rw.

docs.title: Initialising the CLI
docs.text:
This CLI make use of a configuration file, normally placed in the '.datatrail' directory in $HOME. To create a configuration file, use the command datatrail config init --site {SITE}, where {SITE} is 'local', 'chime', or one of the outrigger sites. See below for a guide for each of the sites.

LocalCANFARCHIMEKKOGBOHCO 
# Create Datatrail config file
datatrail config init --site local

# Ensure valid CADC Certificate exists
cadc-get-cert -u [username]

The Configuration steps at CANFAR are similar to the local configuration. One notable exception is that after installation, the datatrail command may not be in the PATH. It is installed to ~/.local/bin, in which case you may need to add it to your PATH or prepend it to the follwing commands.

# Create Datatrail config file
datatrail config init --site canfar

# Ensure valid CADC Certificate exists
cadc-get-cert -u [username]

# Create Datatrail config file
datatrail config init --site chime

# Ensure valid CADC Certificate exists
cadc-get-cert -u [username]

# Create Datatrail config file
datatrail config init --site kko

# Ensure valid CADC Certificate exists
cadc-get-cert -u [username]

# Create Datatrail config file
datatrail config init --site gbo

# Ensure valid CADC Certificate exists
cadc-get-cert -u [username]

# Create Datatrail config file
datatrail config init --site hco

# Ensure valid CADC Certificate exists
cadc-get-cert -u [username]

docs.title: Commands
docs.text:
The commands available to you are:

  • list: This list either the 'scopes' available or all of the datasets belonging to the given dataset.
  • ps: This provides detailed information for the given 'scope' and 'dataset' combination.
  • pull: This allows you to download all files belonging to the 'scope' and 'dataset' provided.
  • clear: This removes all files belonging to the 'scope' and 'dataset', only available for the local and canfar sites.
  • config: Edit the .datatrail/config.yaml configuration file.
  • version: List the CLI and server version.

Detailed information on all of the CLI commands can be found on the Reference page.

docs.title: Datatrail CLI Reference
docs.text: This page provides documentation for the Datatrail CLI.

docs.title: datatrail
docs.text:
Datatrail Command Line Interface.

Usage:

datatrail [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...

Options:

  --help  Show this message and exit.

docs.title: datatrail clear
docs.text:
Clear a dataset.

Usage:

datatrail clear [OPTIONS] SCOPE DATASET

Options:

  -d, --directory DIRECTORY  Directory to clear data from.
  --clear-parents            Clear all empty parent directories of dataset.
  -v, --verbose              Verbosity: v=INFO, vv=DEBUG.
  -q, --quiet                Set log level to ERROR.
  -f, --force                Do not prompt for confirmation.
  --help                     Show this message and exit.

docs.title: datatrail config
docs.text:
Datatrail CLI Configuration.

Usage:

datatrail config [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...

Options:

  --help  Show this message and exit.

docs.title: datatrail config get
docs.text:
Get a configuration value.

Usage:

datatrail config get [OPTIONS] KEY

Options:

  --help  Show this message and exit.

docs.title: datatrail config init
docs.text:
Initialize configuration.

Usage:

datatrail config init [OPTIONS]

Options:

  -s, --site TEXT  Site to initialize.  [required]
  --help           Show this message and exit.

docs.title: datatrail config list
docs.text:
List all configuration values.

Usage:

datatrail config list [OPTIONS]

Options:

  --help  Show this message and exit.

docs.title: datatrail config set
docs.text:
Set a configuration value.

Usage:

datatrail config set [OPTIONS] KEY VALUE

Options:

  --help  Show this message and exit.

docs.title: datatrail list
docs.text:
List scopes & datasets

Usage:

datatrail list [OPTIONS] [SCOPE] [DATASETS]

Options:

  -v, --verbose  Verbosity: v=INFO, vv=DEBUG.
  -q, --quiet    Only errors shown in logs.
  --write        Write the events to file.
  --help         Show this message and exit.

docs.title: datatrail ps
docs.text:
Details of a dataset.

Usage:

datatrail ps [OPTIONS] SCOPE DATASET

Options:

  -s, --show-files  Show file names.
  -v, --verbose     Verbosity: v=INFO, vv=DEBUG.
  -q, --quiet       Set log level to ERROR.
  --help            Show this message and exit.

docs.title: datatrail pull
docs.text:
Download a dataset.

Usage:

datatrail pull [OPTIONS] SCOPE DATASET

Options:

  -d, --directory DIRECTORY  Directory to pull data to.
  -c, --cores INTEGER RANGE  Number of parallel fetch processes to use.
                             [1<=x<=2]
  -v, --verbose              Verbosity: v=INFO, vv=DEBUG.
  -q, --quiet                Set log level to ERROR.
  -f, --force                Do not prompt for confirmation.
  --help                     Show this message and exit.

docs.title: datatrail version
docs.text:
Show versions.

Usage:

datatrail version [OPTIONS]

Options:

  --help  Show this message and exit.
